title: luzula parviflora subsp. parviflora
intro: Luzula parviflora subsp. parviflora, also known as small-flowered woodrush, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Europe, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Luzula parviflora subsp. parviflora include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various conditions. Luzula parviflora subsp. parviflora is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and it can also be used in salves and ointments to treat skin conditions.
